title: All in on AI at Astra
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp suggests he might be the last employee due to the company's strategic shift towards AI. The company previously participated in the SPAC boom.

title: H3 successfully returns to flight
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight on June 11, placing six small satellites into orbit after a previous failure in December. This marks a significant recovery for the rocket program.

title: In aerospace, AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s filling a shortage
summary: Aerospace executives view AI as a solution to labor shortages rather than a replacement for workers, addressing an overstretched industrial base. The industry emphasizes AI's role in augmenting human capabilities amid workforce challenges.

title: AAC Clyde Space wins ESA contract to complete maritime-monitoring constellation
summary: AAC Clyde Space secured a 10.9 million euro contract from the European Space Agency to develop and demonstrate VHF Data Exchange System (VDES) satellites for maritime monitoring. This contract aims to complete the constellation for enhanced maritime communication and tracking.

title: Vandenberg offers new launch site for small and medium rockets
summary: The U.S. Space Force is offering a new launch site at Vandenberg Space Force Base for small and medium rockets. This initiative aims to attract potential users for the facility.

title: Understanding what’s next for orbital data centers
summary: SpaceNews discusses the future of orbital data centers in a recent event, exploring industry interest and technological advancements in this emerging space sector.

title: Senate NDAA backs plan to fold SDA, Space RCO into Space Force
summary: The Senate's 2027 defense bill proposes merging the Space Development Agency (SDA) and Space Rapid Capabilities Office (Space RCO) into the Space Force, eliminating their separate statutory requirements. This move aligns with broader Pentagon procurement reforms.

title: ESA astronaut assignment on Artemis 3 part of negotiations on revised Artemis roles
summary: ESA's astronaut assignment to Artemis 3 is part of ongoing negotiations for revised roles in the Artemis program, aiming to secure European astronauts' participation on the Moon mission.

title: HyImpulse signs letter of intent with Oman’s spaceport
summary: HyImpulse, a German small launch provider, has signed a letter of intent with Oman's Etlaq Spaceport to explore future launch activities and potential operational presence. The agreement aims to facilitate commercial space launches from Oman.

title: Tilebox Launches Verifiable AI Workflows for Satellite Data
summary: Tilebox introduced infrastructure for verifiable AI workflows on Earth observation satellite data, enabling teams to use agents through governed and repeatable processes.

title: Satellite-boosting spacecraft inside air-launched rocket | Space photo of the day for June 12, 2026
summary: A spacecraft was prepared for encapsulation inside a rocket at a NASA launch facility to save another satellite. This mission aims to boost or service an existing satellite in orbit.

title: The critical robot arm on the ISS isn't working properly, but NASA has a plan to fix it
summary: A critical robot arm on the ISS, Canadarm2, broke in May and requires repairs by astronauts starting no earlier than June 30. NASA has a plan to fix it using a spare already available on the station.

title: My quiet obsession with satellites — and how they're ruining everything
summary: The article discusses the negative impacts of satellites on night sky visibility and photography. It highlights how the increasing number of satellites is ruining the darkness and affecting astronomical observations.

title: SpaceX goes public with a mind-bogglingly historic IPO today. The space industry may never be the same.
summary: SpaceX has gone public with a historic IPO today, marking a significant milestone for the space industry. The move could reshape the sector by introducing a major publicly traded company.

title: SpaceX sends 24 Starlink satellites into orbit as market awaits IPO launch (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket. The event heightened anticipation for SpaceX's upcoming NASDAQ IPO.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers 'galaxy-killing' wind that may explain why some early galaxies lived fast and died young
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) and ALMA have discovered a 'galaxy-killing' wind driven by cosmic mergers, which may explain why some early galaxies stopped forming stars prematurely. This finding sheds light on the rapid demise of massive galaxies in the early universe.
company: James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) and ALMA

title: Don't miss this beautiful 3-planet parade after sunset tonight — it won't last long
summary: Mercury, Venus, and Jupiter will form a rare 3-planet alignment low in the western sky after sunset on June 12, offering a brief but stunning celestial display.

title: 'Star City''s Agnes O'Casey and Anna Maxwell Martin spill the tea on Soviet severity, fighting for trousers, and furry hats
summary: Actresses Agnes O'Casey and Anna Maxwell Martin discuss the challenges faced by women in Soviet-era space programs, highlighting themes of resilience and gender equality. The conversation touches on cultural and practical struggles, including clothing and survival in a male-dominated field.

title: Grab our best telescope for beginners with $100 off the Celestron NexStar 4SE
summary: Celestron's NexStar 4SE telescope, ideal for beginners, is on sale with a $100 discount on Amazon. The 4-inch aperture and go-to technology make it a popular choice for new stargazers.

title: Astronauts watch from space as ice splinters from a glacier | Space photo of the day for June 11, 2026
summary: Astronauts observe ice splintering from a glacier in the Southern Patagonia Icefield from space. The changes are visible in a space photo dated June 11, 2026.

title: Black Eye Galaxy
summary: NASA's Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes captured a composite image of the Black Eye Galaxy (Messier 64) in March 2026, combining near-, mid-infrared, ultraviolet, visible, and near-infrared wavelengths.

title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211, resembling a swarm of bees returning to their hive. This observation contributes to ongoing scientific research on galaxy clusters and their formations.

title: World Cup Fever in Guadalajara
summary: Guadalajara's metro area has expanded westward since hosting World Cup matches in 1986, with the landscape shaped by ancient volcanoes.

title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
summary: NASA opened a new funding opportunity called M-STAR to support academic research and technology development. The application window closes on August 11, aiming to advance the agency's Artemis exploration goals.

title: NASA’s Chandra Discovers Possible Supernova Remnant in Galactic Center
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory may have discovered a supernova remnant in the Galactic Center. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, highlight the expanding remains of an exploded star.

title: I Am Artemis: Elkin Norena
summary: Elkin Norena, NASA's Space Launch System Program resident management officer, has overseen more than a dozen space shuttle missions, contributing to historic low Earth orbit launches and station constructions.

title: NASA Robotic Tech Demo Will Advance Prototype Gamma-Ray Detectors
summary: NASA is advancing prototype gamma-ray detectors through a robotic arm demonstration on its upcoming Fly Foundational Robots mission. This new type of gamma-ray sensor aims to enhance space-based gamma-ray observation capabilities.

title: Soccer Meets Space Science
summary: A soccer ball was tested on the International Space Station to study how internal mass affects motion in microgravity. The findings could improve embedded technologies in match balls for better performance.

title: NASA’s Career Technical Education Day Highlights Technical Careers
summary: NASA hosted Career Technical Education Day to highlight technical careers and cultivate the next generation of skilled workers for its missions. The event emphasized the critical role of technicians in advancing the agency's research and innovation.

title: NASA, USGS Scientists Go Rock Hounding in California’s High Desert
summary: NASA and USGS scientists explored California's Mojave Desert to investigate a sensor-detected 'fingerprint' that could indicate topaz deposits. The study aims to uncover geological clues rather than gem-grade treasure.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This crewed mission is part of NASA's Artemis program aimed at returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing on Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Chinese, Rocket Lab, and Starlink flights scheduled
summary: Multiple space launches are scheduled for the upcoming week, including flights from China, Rocket Lab, and Starlink. Three Starlink launches and two other missions are planned from different locations.

title: NASA concludes MAVEN mission at Mars, uses data to discover new atmospheric phenomenon
summary: NASA has concluded the MAVEN mission at Mars, which lasted six months longer than planned. The mission's data has led to the discovery of a new atmospheric phenomenon.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its upcoming Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent New Glenn rocket anomaly during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is continuing development efforts despite the setback.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in advancing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This development marks a key milestone in the spacecraft's design and testing phase.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is assessing damage from an anomaly and identifying future danger areas. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the upcoming Artemis III mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on resilient or advanced technology, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute deployment test is conducted to ensure safe landing. The test is referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out'.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination on Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: VCs Predict the SpaceX IPO Will Lift the Entire Industry
summary: Venture capitalists anticipate that SpaceX's potential IPO will positively impact the broader space industry by boosting investor confidence and attracting more capital. The move could shift the industry's reliance on private funding toward public markets.

title: Op-ed: Up to a Trillion Dollars is About to Chase Space. What Kind of Economy Will Be Built?
summary: A trillion dollars is expected to flow into the space industry, raising questions about how this capital will shape the future space economy. The largest IPO in history could drive record investment, but its impact remains uncertain.

title: What the SpaceX IPO Means for Europe’s Space Industry
summary: SpaceX's IPO is a landmark event for the US space industry, with significant implications for Europe's space sector. The move could reshape global competition and investment dynamics.

title: Exclusive: Tilebox Launches AI-Focused Update
summary: Tilebox has launched an AI-focused update to enhance the effectiveness of AI agents as geospatial-data analysts. The update aims to improve data processing and analysis capabilities in the space industry.

title: Analysts Debate SpaceX’s $1.75T Valuation Ahead of IPO
summary: Analysts are divided on SpaceX's $1.75 trillion valuation ahead of its potential IPO. The debate highlights the booming growth of the space industry.

title: Symphony Space Unveils Adagio-XL ODC Sat
summary: Symphony Space has introduced the Adagio-XL ODC Sat, entering the orbital data center market. The company aims to leverage this new technology for space-based data processing.

title: Orbital Raises $5M Pre-Seed to Build ODC Constellation
summary: Orbital has raised $5 million in a pre-seed funding round to develop its ODC constellation. The ODC concept uses a network of smaller satellites to handle large AI inference workloads and maintain constant ground communication.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo More Time to Launch
summary: The FCC has extended the deadline for Amazon's Project Kuiper to deploy at least half of its satellite constellation, while imposing conditions to expedite launches.

title: Artemis III Astronauts Will Meet SpaceX, Blue Origin Landers in Earth Orbit
summary: NASA confirmed that SpaceX and Blue Origin will each provide a lander for the Artemis III mission's Earth orbit test in 2027, serving as a precursor to future Moon landings. The event marked a key step in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Reflex Aerospace Taps Arkadia Space for 2027 Mission
summary: Reflex Aerospace selected Spanish startup Arkadia Space to provide a propulsion system for its satellite mission, launching in 2027.

title: Satellite IoT is Becoming Essential Infrastructure as NTN and Cellular IoT Services Converge
summary: Satellite IoT is gaining importance as Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTN) and cellular IoT services merge, becoming critical infrastructure for enterprises managing fleets, utilities, and remote assets. The integration ensures continuous connectivity, proving essential for real-time operations.

title: U.S. State Department Taps SpaceX’s Starlink for Two-Year Disaster Response Initiative
summary: The U.S. Department of State signed a two-year agreement with SpaceX to use Starlink for improved disaster response and humanitarian aid. This initiative aims to leverage Starlink's satellite internet for global emergency preparedness.

title: Canadian Government Awards EO Satellite Contracts to Calian, Kepler, MDA Space
summary: The Canadian government awarded three Earth Observation (EO) satellite contracts totaling C$2.4 million to Calian, Kepler, and MDA Space to enhance its EO capabilities.

title: Viasat Secures PTS-G U.S. Space Force SSC Swarm 1 Contract
summary: Viasat has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command to build, launch, and deliver the first satellite in a proliferated fleet as part of the PTS-G Swarm 1 mission. This contract marks a significant step in Viasat's involvement in U.S. military space initiatives.

title: SpaceX Set for IPO, Banking on Faith in Elon Musk and Massive AI Growth
summary: SpaceX is preparing for its IPO on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SPCX, leveraging investor confidence in Elon Musk and the anticipated growth of AI. The company, which has operated privately for 24 years, aims to capitalize on this milestone.

title: Applied Atomics Emerges from Stealth with Enough Backing to Build Star Reacher Network
summary: Applied Atomics has exited stealth mode with over $500 million in demand commitments to develop the Star Reacher network, a mobility infrastructure layer.

title: Space Forge Secures £10 Million of New Investment through European, UK Space Agencies
summary: Space Forge has secured £10 million ($13.4 million) in new investment from the European Space Agency (ESA) and the UK Space Agency (UKSA). The funding is part of the ESA’s General Support Technology Programme, supported by UKSA’s investment.

title: Lithuania and Luxembourg Strengthen Space Defense Ties
summary: Lithuania and Luxembourg are strengthening their cooperation in space defense following a meeting between their respective defense ministers. The collaboration aims to enhance mutual security efforts in the space domain.

title: Another Earth Expands Into Brazil
summary: Another Earth, an Earth Observation (EO) data company, is expanding into Brazil, one of Latin America's largest markets. The expansion is driven by Brazil's climate pressures and rapid growth.

title: Airbus to Lead German Consortium for Sovereign ISR Capability
summary: Airbus is leading a German consortium to develop a sovereign intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) solution. The announcement was made during the Berlin Air Show, involving multiple German companies.
